content.problemStatement: Given an integer xxx, find two non-negative integers aaa and bbb such that (a∧b)+(a∨b)=x(a \wedge b) + (a \vee b) = x(a∧b)+(a∨b)=x, where ∧\wedge∧ is the bitwise AND operation and ∨\vee∨ is the bitwise OR operation.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer x.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output two space-separated non-negative integers a and b such that (a & b) + (a | b) = x. Any valid pair is accepted.

solution.approach: The key observation is the identity (a & b) + (a | b) = a + b for any non‑negative integers a and b. Therefore we only need to find any pair (a,b) with a+b = x. The simplest choice is a = 0 and b = x, which always satisfies the condition. Hence for each test case we just output "0 x". This runs in O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) total, O(1) per test case.

code.Python3:
import sys
data=sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
if not data:
    sys.exit()
T=int(data[0])
out=[]
idx=1
for _ in range(T):
    x=data[idx]
    idx+=1
    out.append(f"0 {x}")
sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))
